mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tight labor market conditions that support corporate pricing power and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production above 110 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110, or the 60d return volatility increases by >20% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 200 in the CPIAUCSL index triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns becomes positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions that drive equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST exceed their thresholds, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows negative coefficient in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening regime characterized by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and persistently high CPIAUCSL above 200 leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed their thresholds, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows positive coefficient in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 130,000 in PAYEMS signals expanding employment that supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130,000, or the 5d return volatility increases by >15% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in residential construction (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for real estate, boosting equity markets and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST exceed their thresholds, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows negative coefficient in regression.
